data.title: DocMorris Shares Plunge After Loss
data.description:
By Helena Smolak

DocMorris shares plunged after it posted a full-year loss on higher marketing costs to boost its digital prescription sales in Germany.

In European early morning trade, shares of DocMorris fell 21% to 16.60 Swiss francs, its steepest share drop since the company started trading in 2017. The stock is down 78% in the last 12 months.

The Switzerland-listed online pharmacy said Thursday that the adjusted loss before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--the company's preferred key metric and closely watched by investors--widened to 48.6 million francs from 34.9 million francs in the prior-year period on higher marketing costs.

Its net loss from continuing operations narrowed to 97.3 million euros from a loss of 117.6 million francs.

Net revenue increased to 1.02 billion francs from 969.5 million francs on a higher customer base that rose to 10.3 million at the end of 2024 from 9.1 million the year before. Since April 2024--when Germany introduced digital prescriptions--DocMorris's prescription business has grown continuously, the company said.

For 2025, the company expects sales growth of around 50% in the first quarter of 2025. The company plans a capital increase of around 200 million Swiss francs.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com

data.title: Acer Board Approves Cash Dividend of NT$1.7/Shr >2353.TW
HPL Electric & Power Secures New Orders Worth INR3.7 Billion; Shares Jump 7%
data.description:
HPL Electric & Power has bagged orders worth 3.70 billion Indian rupees from its regular undisclosed customers, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The orders are for the supply of smart meters.

Shares of the company were up nearly 7% in recent trade.

data.title: American Axle-Dowlais Merger Advances with US Antitrust Clearance
data.description:
The US Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust Improvements Act waiting period has expired for the proposed merger of American Axle & Manufacturing Holdings and Dowlais Group , according to a Thursday filing.

Regulatory filings in other jurisdictions are progressing, and the combination of the UK-listed automotive-focused specialist engineering group with the US-listed automotive components company is expected to close in the fourth quarter, pending the satisfaction of the remaining conditions.

data.title: Market Chatter: China Says US Weakened Anti-Drug Cooperation By Slapping Tariffs
data.description:
The Chinese government said the US has weakened bilateral cooperation against drugs by imposing tariffs against Beijing, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing a senior foreign ministry official.

The official called on the US to stop using the fentanyl issue to "blackmail" the country, following the increase of tariffs against Chinese goods by the US to 20% from 10%, the report said.

China's remarks come as it presented its white paper on the fentanyl issue released earlier in March, according to the report.

"The United States doesn't appreciate this kindness. On the contrary, it is using the fentanyl issue to spread all kinds of lies and has been smearing China, shifting the blame, regardless of the progress of the cooperation," Reuters quoted the official as saying.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Lotus Bakeries Shares Rise on Expansion to Ice Cream Products
data.description:
By Maitane Sardon

Lotus Bakeries shares rose after it said it expanded its existing agreement with food major Mondelez International to include ice cream products.

Shares were up 5.2% at 8,450 euros in early European trade Thursday.

Under the deal, major ice cream maker Froneri will now produce Lotus Biscoff-branded ice cream products, the Belgium snack food company said.

"This collaboration will strengthen our position in the ice cream sector, enabling Biscoff to grow into a global ice-cream brand. Moreover, introducing the Biscoff taste to icecream consumers worldwide will further boost our brand awareness and the ability of our own organization to focus on Biscoff cookies and spread globally." Lotus Bakeries Chief Executive Jan Boone said.

Write to Maitane Sardon at maitane.sardon@wsj.com

data.title: K33 AB (publ): Primary Insider Share Purchase Notice
data.description: Muunilinst AS, which is closely associated with Torbjørn Bull Jenssen, CEO of K33 AB (publ), acquired 1,000,000 shares in K33 AB (publ) on March 13, 2025, at a price of SEK 0.04 per share. Following this acquisition, Torbjørn Bull Jenssen and Muunilinst AS together hold a total of 280,776,596 shares in K33 AB (publ).

data.title: MAC Copper Lands New Debt Facilities
data.description:
MAC Copper said late Wednesday it has secured new debt facilities consisting of a $159 million term loan facility, an upsized $125 million revolving credit facility extended to March 14, 2028, and a 45 million-Australian-dollar ($28.3 million) environmental bond now provided by three Australian banks.

The company's old facilities comprised a $159 million term loan facility, an undrawn $25 million revolver, a $145 million mezzanine facility and a A$45 million environmental bond.

MAC said it exercised its right to fully repay its mezzanine debt facility with Sprott by paying it $160.1 million.

The company's shares were up 1.6% in recent premarket activity Thursday.

data.title: AlloVir Shareholders Approve Merger With Kalaris Therapeutics
data.description:
AlloVir shareholders have approved the company's proposed merger with Kalaris Therapeutics at a special meeting held Wednesday, the companies said the same day.

The merger is expected to close "as soon as practicable" and the combined company will be renamed as Kalaris Therapeutics, with "KLRS" as its updated trading symbol on Nasdaq.

data.title: Futu Holdings Q4 Earnings, Revenue Increase
data.description:
Futu Holdings reported Q4 earnings Thursday of 13.35 Hong Kong dollars ($1.72) per American depositary share, up from HK$6.31 a year earlier.

Three analysts polled by FactSet expected HK$12.93.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was HK$4.43 billion, up from HK$2.37 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected HK$3.83 billion.

The company's shares were up more than 2% in recent Thursday premarket activity.

data.title: Sale of Colombian unit lifts Telefonica shares
data.description:
** Shares of Telefonica rise 1.6% to the top of Madrid's IBEX35 index after the Spanish telecom said it signed a definitive agreement to sell its Colombian unit to Millicom for $400 million

** The deal is part of Telefonica's strategy to gradually reduce its exposure to Latin America

** Brokerage ING believes it is a good transaction, although it will have only a small impact on Telefonica's financial profile

** ING adds, however, that Telefonica may benefit from a greater strategic focus on more profitable markets as well as a reduced exposure to non-euro currencies

** Millicom's Stockholm-listed shares are up 1.8% in morning trade

data.title: UK's IG Group rises after 12% jump in quarterly revenue
data.description:
** British online trading platform IG Group up 4.3% at 965p; stock among top pct gainers on FTSE 250 index

** Reports 12% jump in Q3 revenue to 268 million pounds ($347.33 million) helped by more active clients

** Expects to meet consensus expectations for fiscal 2025

** For fiscal 2025 co expected to post revenue of 1.03 billion pounds and adjusted pre-tax profit of 494 million pounds, according to a company-compiled consensus

** IGG down ~2% YTD

($1 = 0.7716 pounds)

data.title: India's HDFC Life says earlier data theft had no 'material impact'
data.description:
India's HDFC Life Insurance said on Thursday that a data theft it reported in November did not result in any adverse material impact and has been resolved.

"The unauthorized exploitation was confined to a specific, identified issue," the insurer said in an exchange filing, without disclosing further details.

HDFC Life, in November, said that an unknown source shared certain customer data with the company with "mala fide intent".

data.title: BOE Could Have Three More Interest Rate Cuts in 2025, Says Investec — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of England is likely to maintain a gradual pace of interest-rate cuts, making three more cuts in 2025 of 25 basis points each, Investec Economics' Sandra Horsfield says in a note. The impact of U.S. steel and aluminium tariffs could negatively affect U.K. businesses and push down inflation. Increased uncertainty around the implementation of the tariffs could cause businesses to hold off on investing which could also push down inflation, she says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Acer 4Q Rev TWD66.02B >2353.TW
Acer 4Q EPS TWD0.47 >2353.TW
French Stocks Fall as Tariff Woes Persist
data.description:
The CAC 40 fell 0.4% to 7,957 on Thursday, reversing the gains from the previous session, as escalating global trade tensions continued to weigh on market sentiment.

U.S. President Donald Trump reaffirmed his intention to impose additional tariffs after the steel and aluminum duties sparked retaliatory measures from the European Union and Canada, once again warning of reciprocal tariffs set to take effect next month.

The luxury sector was under pressure, with L'Oréal dropping 1.8%, LVMH down 1.5% to its lowest point in over three months, Kering falling 0.9%, and Hermès losing 0.8%.

In contrast, the financial sector showed strength, with AXA climbing 2.1%, BNP Paribas up 1%, Crédit Agricole rising 0.6%, and Société Générale gaining 0.4%.

data.title: FIT Hon Teng Logs Higher Profit, Revenue in 2024
data.description:
FIT Hon Teng recorded a higher profit attributable to owners for 2024 of $153.7 million compared with $129.0 million a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share were $0.0217, up from $0.0182 a year earlier.

Revenue saw a surge of 6.1% to $4.45 billion from $4.20 billion a year earlier.

The company's shares were down over 9% at the close of trading.

data.title: A10 Networks Plans Private Offering of $200 Million of Convertible Notes
data.description:
A10 Networks said late Wednesday it plans to launch a private offering to eligible purchasers of $200 million of convertible senior notes due 2030.

The company intends to grant the initial purchasers a 13-day option to purchase up to $25 million additional amount of notes.

Net proceeds will be used for share repurchases, potential acquisitions or investments, working capital and general corporate purposes, the company said.

data.title: Lotte Chemical to resume production at EVA plant
data.description: Lotte Chemical is set to restart its Ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA) plant in Daesan.A Polymerupdate source in South Korea informed a team member,

data.description:

data.title: Crisil Keeps Unimech Aerospace's Credit Rating at A-; Outlook Revised to Positive
data.description:
Crisil affirmed its A- corporate credit rating on Unimech Aerospace and Manufacturing , according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

The rating outlook was revised to positive from stable, the filing said.

data.title: Felleskjøpet Agri SA - Successful placement of new senior unsecured bonds
data.description:
Felleskjøpet Agri SA (rated BBB-/stable by Scope Ratings) has successfullyraised NOK 1,000 million across 3 tranches with tenors of 3, 5 and 7 years. o New senior unsecured 3-year NOK 250 million bond issue with a coupon of3months Nibor + 1.45%, o New senior unsecured 5-year NOK 500 million bond issue with a couponof 3months Nibor + 1.75%, ando New senior unsecured 7-year NOK 250 million with a fixed coupon of6.05%

The bond issues will be applied for listing on Nordic ABM.

In conjunction with the potential bond issuance, Felleskjøpet Agri SA hasboughtback NOK 137 million of its outstanding senior unsecured bond FKOV14 PRO due29December 2025 (with ISIN: NO0010915374) and NOK 292 million of its outstandingsenior unsecured bond FKOV15 PRO due 17 January 2026 (with ISIN:NO0011202194).

DNB Markets and Nordea acted as Joint Bookrunners for the transaction.

For further information, please contact: Head of Treasury Felleskjøpet Agri SA, Per Olaf Stampe tel. +47 92 06 00 17

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/641100

data.title: Russian rouble steadies close to over six-month high vs dollar
data.description:
The Russian rouble steadied close to a more than six-month high against the dollar on Thursday as the market waited for the next development on the Ukraine war after Kyiv said it was ready to support Washington's 30-day ceasefire proposal.

The Kremlin said on Wednesday it would review details from Washington about the proposed ceasefire in Ukraine before responding, while U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio hoped a deal would be struck within days.

By 0824 GMT, the rouble was up 0.8% at 86.45 to the dollar in over-the-counter market trade. It had reached 85.1205, its strongest level since August 7, 2024, on Tuesday.

Against the Chinese yuan, the most traded foreign currency in Russia, the rouble was up 0.4% to 11.88.

The rouble is up against the dollar this year, mostly thanks to expectations of improved relations between Moscow and Washington.

The currency's strengthening could have some bearing on the central bank when it next meets to set interest rates.

Anatoly Aksakov, head of the financial committee in Russia's lower house of parliament, the State Duma, said he expected the central bank to keep rates at 21% on March 21, with high inflation preventing the central bank from being able to ease borrowing costs.

"On the other hand, we see the rouble exchange rate is strengthening and there are forecasts that it will strengthen even further," Aksakov said at a financial forum on Thursday. "Respectively, the political factor is influencing the fact that the key rate may be lowered."

Brent crude oil LCOc1, a global benchmark for Russia's main export, was up 0.4% at $71.22 a barrel.

About us

Our purpose is to make a difference by developing natural resources, improving people's lives now and for generations to come. We are trusted by our owners and partners to realise the potential of their resources. We produce minerals and metals critical to the world's energy transition from operations across the Americas, Australia and Southern Africa and we are discovering and responsibly developing our next generation of mines. We aspire to leave a positive legacy and build meaningful relationships with our partners and communities to create brighter futures together.

data.title: Melco to Pick Up Stake in Security Solutions Firm
data.description:
Melco Holdings is forming a capital and business alliance with Secure through share allocation, a security solutions business, according to a Thursday bourse filing.

Under the deal, Melco will purchase 800,000 shares in Secure at 1,819 yen each, totaling 1.46 billion yen, equating to 14.35% voting rights post-allocation.
